production_note: agentic_K1 dual-LLM skeptic: sealed receipt write; packet-only; no bulk writers; no identity stamps
knowledge_boundary: Only landing + prior + active packet content.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"], "score": [0.5, 0.8]})
df = df.set_index("cliente_id")
print(df.index.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: set_index cliente_id → index labels del contrato.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.Series([0.1, 0.9], index=["C001", "C002"], name="score")
print(s["C002"])
exercises.justification_from_packet: Una Series del theory T1-A es vector con Index: acceso por etiqueta del índice devuelve 0.9 en el fixture de scores, no por posición ciega.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s1 = pd.Series([1.0, 2.0], index=["C001", "C002"])
s2 = pd.Series([0.5], index=["C002"])
print(s1.add(s2, fill_value=0).sort_index().to_dict())
exercises.justification_from_packet: add fill_value=0 alinea índices; C001=1.0 C002=2.5.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO("a,b\n1,NA\n2,3\n"), na_values=["NA"])
print(int(df["b"].isna().sum()))
exercises.justification_from_packet: read_csv con na_values marca 'NA' como nulo (T1-B); en la columna b del fixture queda exactamente 1 null antes de imputar.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO("fecha,v\n2024-01-01,1\n"), parse_dates=["fecha"])
print(str(df["fecha"].dtype))
exercises.justification_from_packet: parse_dates del parser CSV convierte la columna fecha a datetime64[ns], como documenta la lectura CSV/Excel de T1-B.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO("cliente_id,monto,z\nC001,1.0,9\n"), usecols=["cliente_id", "monto"])
print(df.columns.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: usecols limita la ingesta a las columnas necesarias: cliente_id y monto, reduciendo memoria y ruido según opciones de parser T1-B.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"], "score": [0.4, 0.9]})
print(df.loc[df.score >= 0.5, "cliente_id"].tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: loc combina máscara booleana y etiqueta de columna (T2-A): score>=0.5 selecciona cliente_id → ['C002'] en el fixture.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [1.0, 2.0]})
print(df.assign(doble=df["score"] * 2)["doble"].tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: assign crea columnas derivadas sin mutar a ciegas: doble=score*2 produce [2.0,4.0] alineado al contrato de filtros/assign T2-A.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
print(int(df.iloc[1, 0]))
exercises.justification_from_packet: iloc es puramente posicional (fila 1, col 0 → 3); no usa etiquetas del Index, contraste explícito con loc en T2-A.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [0.2, 0.9]}).copy()
df.loc[:, "flag"] = ""
df.loc[df.score < 0.5, "flag"] = "x"
print(df["flag"].fillna("").tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: loc + fillna('') → ['x','']. Evita SettingWithCopy.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [0.2, 0.9, 0.7]})
sub = df.loc[df.score > 0.5].copy()
sub["ok"] = True
print(sub["ok"].tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Tras filtrar, .copy() + asignación de ok=True evita SettingWithCopy y deja ambas filas True, como recomienda copy semantics T2-B.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [1.0, 2.0]})
c = df.copy()
c.loc[0, "score"] = 99.0
print(df["score"].tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Mutar la copia del filtro no altera el DataFrame original: scores siguen [1.0,2.0]; es el anti-patrón de chained assignment de T2-B.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"region": ["lima", "cusco"]})
df["region"] = df["region"].str.title().astype("category")
print(df["region"].dtype.name)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Convertir a category tras title() reduce cardinalidad de strings (T3-A); dtype resultante es 'category' para la columna de títulos.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.to_numeric(pd.Series(["1", "a", "3"]), errors="coerce")
print([float(x) if pd.notna(x) else float("nan") for x in s])
exercises.justification_from_packet: to_numeric(..., errors='coerce') transforma inválidos a NaN sin tumbar el pipeline, política de coerción de T3-A/T3-B.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.to_datetime(pd.Series(["2024-01-01", "no-fecha"]), errors="coerce")
print(int(s.isna().sum()))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Un parse de fecha inválido introduce NaT; el conteo de nulos/NaT da 1 en el fixture, coherente con dtypes datetime de T3-A.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
raw = pd.Series(["1.0", "x", "2.0"])
num = pd.to_numeric(raw, errors="coerce")
print(int(num.isna().sum() - raw.isna().sum()))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Schema/coerción explícita: un valor no parseable introduce 1 NaN nuevo antes de fallar en silencio; T3-B pide contarlo.
Si falta una columna del schema objetivo se reporta 'missing' (fail-closed), no se inventa con defaults (T3-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"]})
df["cliente_id"] = df["cliente_id"].astype("string")
print(str(df["cliente_id"].dtype))
exercises.justification_from_packet: astype('string') produce StringDtype nullable del theory T3-A/B, distinto de object genérico con None mezclados.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1], "b": [2]})
buf = StringIO()
df.to_csv(buf, index=False)
buf.seek(0)
print(pd.read_csv(buf).columns.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Round-trip to_csv/read_csv sin index conserva columnas ['a','b'] del contrato de exportación T4-A; no reintroducir Unnamed:0.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import BytesIO
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1]})
bio = BytesIO()
df.to_excel(bio, index=False, engine="openpyxl")
print(len(bio.getvalue()) > 0)
exercises.justification_from_packet: to_excel con engine openpyxl escribe un buffer con bytes>0 → True; verifica que la exportación no quedó vacía (T4-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
print({c: str(df[c].dtype) for c in sorted(df.columns)})
exercises.justification_from_packet: El contrato de dtypes por columna se valida ordenado frente al schema; cualquier drift se reporta antes de promover el artefacto (T4-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1], "b": ["x"]})
print(int(df.memory_usage(deep=True).sum()) > 0)
exercises.justification_from_packet: memory_usage(deep=True).sum() > 0 documenta footprint real del DataFrame en el manifest de provenance T4-B.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3]})
manifest = {"rows": len(df), "columns": df.columns.tolist()}
print(manifest["rows"], manifest["columns"])
exercises.justification_from_packet: Manifest del gate: rows=3 y columns=['a'] del fixture; registra forma y columnas para auditoría (T4-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd, hashlib
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1]})
print(hashlib.sha1(df.to_csv(index=False).encode()).hexdigest()[:8])
exercises.justification_from_packet: Hash sha1 de to_csv truncado a 8 hex (462f48ef) fija provenance del snapshot; T4-B exige evidencia reproducible del artefacto.

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: loc usa etiquetas de index/columnas; iloc es posicional.
SettingWithCopy: asignación sobre slices view/copy ambiguos.
Theory T3-A/B: errors='coerce' en to_numeric convierte valores inválidos a NaN en lugar de lanzar; permite medir nulos nuevos del schema.
Manifest: filas, columnas y provenance/hash del artefacto.
